content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 3, 1988


Mary A. Donovan
Executive Secretary
State Ethics Commission
One North Capitol, Suite 444
Indianapolis, IN 46204-2026

Re: Your Request for Information
Our File No. G-88-378

Dear Ms. Donovan:

You have requested information concerning California laws
on nepotism. Enclosed is a copy of the Political Reform Act of
1974 (the "Act").1/ Sections 87100 and 87103 prohibit any
state or local public official from participating in any
governmental decision which would have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect on any member of the official's
immediate family. An official's "immediate family" is the
official's spouse and dependent children. (Section 82029.)
Regulation 18702.1(a)(4) and (c) (copy enclosed) specifies when
an effect on a member of an official's immediate family is
"material."

There may be other California statutes concerning nepotism,
however they would not be administered or enforced by the Fair
Political Practices Commission. We suggest that you contact
the office of the California Attorney General concerning other
laws.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:plh

Enclosure

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

STATE ~ INDIANA

INDIANAPOLIS, 46204

STATE ETHICS COMMISSION
ONE NORTH CAPITOL, SUITE 444
INDIANAPOLIS, INDIANA 46204-2026

August 29, 1988


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

We have been asked to review our own Nepotism statute including researching other states' statutes, regulations, or agency rulings concerning nepotism.

I would greatly appreciate any information you can send me.

Thank you.


Sincerely,

Mary A. Donovan
Executive Secretary

sections.conclusion: Sections 87100 and 87103 prohibit any state or local public official from participating in any governmental decision which would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any member of the official's immediate family. An official's 'immediate family' is the official's spouse and dependent children. Regulation 18702.1(a)(4) and (c) specifies when an effect on a member of an official's immediate family is 'material.' Other California nepotism statutes may exist but would not be administered or enforced by the FPPC.
sections.question_synthetic: What are California's laws on nepotism, particularly regarding public officials' participation in governmental decisions affecting their immediate family members?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: California law prohibits public officials from participating in decisions affecting their immediate family members (spouse and dependent children) when there would be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect. The FPPC administers sections 87100 and 87103 of the Political Reform Act, but other nepotism statutes may exist under different agencies' jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of incoming request (August 29, 1988) followed by FPPC response (October 3, 1988). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable. File number G-88-378 indicates this is the FPPC's official case file.

QUESTION: What are California's laws on nepotism, particularly regarding public officials' participation in governmental decisions affecting their immediate family members?

CONCLUSION: Sections 87100 and 87103 prohibit any state or local public official from participating in any governmental decision which would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any member of the official's immediate family. An official's 'immediate family' is the official's spouse and dependent children. Regulation 18702.1(a)(4) and (c) specifies when an effect on a member of an official's immediate family is 'material.' Other California nepotism statutes may exist but would not be administered or enforced by the FPPC.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to an inquiry from the Indiana State Ethics Commission regarding California's nepotism laws by providing information on sections 87100 and 87103 of the Political Reform Act and relevant regulations governing conflicts of interest involving public officials' immediate family members.
